tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>
Remember that my favorite programming language is TypeScript.

response.delta: The user wants me to remember their favorite programming language. I'll save this as a memory.

content.text: I've got some great hotel recommendations. Let me compile the best ones across different categories and price ranges, then save them as a memory.

Here are some excellent LA hotel recommendations across different budgets and neighborhoods! Let me save these for you too.

---

### 🏨 **Luxury / Splurge**

1. **Hotel Bel-Air** —

Tucked away in one of Beverly Hills' most exclusive hillside neighborhoods, it has sprawling verdant grounds and airy, ultra-private rooms. Dating back to the 1940s, it remains a paragon of old Hollywood glamour.
Rooms from $1,183/night.
2. **The Beverly Hills Hotel** —
This Dorchester Collection icon has a red carpet running up its entrance steps and is the quintessential Los Angeles hotel.
Standard rooms start around $1,145/night.
3. **Chateau Marmont** —

If it's Hollywood history you're after, there is no better place. It's chic in a way that Hollywood rarely is, with 63 rooms and suites kitted out with vintage teak furniture and 1930s tiled bathrooms.
Rooms from $1,055.
4. **The Maybourne Beverly Hills** —
Operated by the same group behind Claridge's and The Connaught in London, it features 205 redesigned guest rooms with mid-century furniture and collectible artworks.

### 🌊 **Beach / Santa Monica**

5. **Casa del Mar** —
Runs $692–$1,606/night for direct beach access that only two LA hotels can claim.
A bougie hotel showcasing Italian Renaissance Revival-style in full flight.
6. **Hotel Oceana Santa Monica** —
Features expansive suites with coastal-inspired decor and private balconies, with personalized service, wellness programming, and bespoke dining.
7. **The Georgian** —
Striking Art Deco architecture meets modern sophistication at this historic 1930s coastal landmark, with curated art exhibitions, vibrant live music and bespoke local collaborations.

### 🏙️ **Mid-Range / Great Value**

8. **The London West Hollywood** —

Offers the best luxury-to-value ratio in West Hollywood. All 200 rooms measure at least 750 sq ft (the largest in the city), with private balconies, wet bars, Italian linens, and a rooftop pool with panoramic views.
9. **Hotel Figueroa (Downtown)** —
One of the longest-standing hotels in Downtown LA, it was dazzlingly restored in Spanish Colonial splendor, with 268 beautifully appointed rooms and standout shared spaces.
Rates run $200–$400/night, one of the better values in central LA.
10. **1 Hotel West Hollywood** —

Perched above the Sunset Strip, it transitions from sunlit serenity to vibrant nightlife, with a rooftop pool offering sweeping city-to-ocean views. Its nature-driven design and wellness focus make it a go-to for locals and visitors.

### 🆕 **New for 2026**

11. **PUBLIC West Hollywood** —
One of six new LA hotels in early 2026, Ian Schrager's PUBLIC West Hollywood is in the former Standard building.
12. **The Anthem Hotel** —
A 179-room property that opened in February 2026 as the first lifestyle hotel in LA's Stadium District, near Intuit Dome, SoFi Stadium, and Kia Forum.

---

Now let me save these recommendations for you!

params.title: LA Hotel Recommendations - Trip Next Week (June 2026)
params.content:
Hotel recommendations for upcoming LA trip:

LUXURY:
1. Hotel Bel-Air — Old Hollywood glamour, secluded canyon location, rooms from $1,183/night
2. The Beverly Hills Hotel — Iconic Dorchester Collection property, rooms from $1,145/night
3. Chateau Marmont — Historic, chic, vintage teak furniture, rooms from $1,055/night
4. The Maybourne Beverly Hills — Mid-century design, Whisky Bar, rooftop Dante restaurant

BEACH / SANTA MONICA:
5. Casa del Mar — Direct beach access, Italian Renaissance Revival style, $692–$1,606/night
6. Hotel Oceana Santa Monica — Expansive suites, coastal decor, wellness programming
7. The Georgian — Art Deco 1930s landmark, live music, curated art exhibitions

MID-RANGE / GREAT VALUE:
8. The London West Hollywood — Best luxury-to-value, all rooms 750+ sq ft, rooftop pool
9. Hotel Figueroa (Downtown) — Spanish Colonial charm, $200–$400/night, great value
10. 1 Hotel West Hollywood — Nature-driven design, rooftop pool, Sunset Strip location

NEW FOR 2026:
11. PUBLIC West Hollywood — Ian Schrager property in former Standard building
12. The Anthem Hotel — Near SoFi Stadium, opened Feb 2026

TIP: Pick neighborhood based on purpose — Beverly Hills for shopping/meetings, Santa Monica for beach, Downtown for arts/dining, West Hollywood for nightlife.

anthropic.citedText: The trick is matching the neighborhood to your purpose. Beverly Hills works for shopping and industry meetings. Santa Monica delivers beach access and...

response.delta: The trick is matching the neighborhood to your purpose. Beverly Hills works
for shopping and industry meetings. Santa Monica delivers beach access and walking distance to Third
Street Promenade. Downtown LA now has legitimate options near Walt Disney Concert Hall and The
Broad, while West Hollywood has restaurants, nightlife, and the Sunset Strip.
Would you like me to dig
deeper into any specific neighborhood, budget range, or hotel? I can also look up availability
or dining options near any of these! 🌴
